Title: Netanyahu continues war against Hezbollah in Lebanon despite Iran ceasefire
Subhead: The Israeli prime minister stressed that Donald Trump's truce does not apply to Lebanon, contradicting his Pakistani counterpart, who is mediating the conflict and said the suspension of hostilities is meant to apply 'everywhere.'

Title: Trump blasts NATO after closed-door meeting with Secretary-General Rutte
Subhead: The US president accused its allies of 'turning their back' on the American people after Washington's partners refused to back his war on Iran. After his meeting on Wednesday, he attacked NATO on social media and appeared to renew threats over Greenland.

Title: As the oil shock hits, China's truck drivers face the threat of layoffs: 'Under these conditions, we might as well leave the truck in the parking lot'
Subhead: Fearing a loss of competitiveness for their products, transport companies remain highly reluctant to pass on to their customers fuel cost increases that have been triggered by the war in Iran and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

Title: Emmanuel Grégoire launches the first four major initiatives of his term as mayor of Paris
Subhead: After-school care, housing, sheltering people living on the streets, Parc des Princes: The Socialist mayor is convening a Paris City Council meeting on April 14 to implement the top priorities of his program.

Title: Residents of PFAS-polluted areas turn anger into political action in Brussels
Subhead: For the first time since the 'forever chemicals' scandal came to light, residents from contamination hotspots across Europe gathered in Brussels in early March to take part in political action training. Le Monde followed that historic moment.

In Hungary, Vance backs Orban in last-ditch attempt five days before elections
Subhead: The American vice president praised the Hungarian prime minister during a rally in Budapest on Tuesday. In power since 2010, the nationalist leader is not the favorite in the April 12 legislative elections.

Title: 'A global club in a global city, PSG has every interest in staying at the Parc des Princes'
Subhead: New Paris Mayor Emmanuel Grégoire has reopened talks with the Parisian club, suggesting the team could stay in its historic stadium, according to Jérôme Latta.

Title: 'He said if the kids pulled off the job, there'd be many more': The strange foiled attack on Bank of America in Paris
Subhead: Three teenagers and a 21-year-old man were placed in detention as part of the investigation into the attempted bombing of Bank of America's office in Paris. Investigators suspect that Iranian intelligence services were behind the plot.
